title: grindelia stricta var. collina
intro: Grindelia stricta var. collina, also known as gumweed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes. The health benefits of grindelia stricta var. collina include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and promote wound healing. Grindelia stricta var. collina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in salves and ointments to promote skin health and reduce inflammation.
